DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT
Off Calendar Jan-27-2025 Overruled
MINI MINUTES:

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT CLOUD9 DISCOVERY LLC's DEMURRER to Amended COMPLAINT.

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendant Cloud9's demurrer to the fourth cause of action for breach of contract in the third amended complaint is overruled.

Cloud9's prior demurrer based on CCP Section 430.10(g) was overruled and may not be re-asserted. Cloud9's argument that new circumstances mandate a reconsideration of this argument is unpersuasive given that plaintiff Optrics' new allegations have nothing to do with the basis for the prior ruling.

In paragraph105 of the TAC plaintiff added the allegation that "Terreri and/or Neibaron were unfamiliar with electronic discovery matters, and were therefore unable to negotiate on Optrics' behalf with BNI's counsel" (TAC, Para 105.) This is sufficient to meet the intent standard outlined in Goonewardene for a third-party beneficiary theory because it shows that Cloud9 and Terreri and/or Neibaron entered into this agreement intending to benefit Plaintiff. The new allegation necessarily implies the contract was only created once Defendants Terreri and/or Neibaron realized they were insufficiently competent regarding the e-discovery in the underlying lawsuit.

Defendant's assertion that the TAC does not properly allege other elements of a breach of contract claim also lack merit. The allegations in the TAC sufficiently allege a breach of contract claim against Cloud9.

Order submitted.

Judge: Harold E. Kahn; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Not reported. (301/HEK)

Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint
Off Calendar Oct-18-2024 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances (via Zoom videoconference):
Atty. UJVALA SINGH, Esq. of Law Office of Ujvala Singh (tel: 415-791-8198) for Plaintiff.
Atty. CHRISTOPHER SWISHER, Esq. of Zelms Erlich & Mack (tel: 480-608-2114) for Defendants HERBERT L. TERRERI, ESQ. and LAW OFFICES OF HERBERT L. TERRERI, APC.
Atty. ANTOINETTE HEWITT and Atty. SHELBY MORBACH, Esq. of Kutak Rock LLP (tel: 949-417-0999) for Defendant Cloud9 Discovery LLC.

Hearing Re: DEFENDANT LAW OFFICES OF HERBERT L. TERRERI, APC, AND HERBERT TERRERI, ESQ's Motion For Leave To File Cross-Complaint.

Matter argued and submitted. Counsel Ujvala Singh requests the Court waive the meet and confer requirements for future demurrers and motions to strike in this matter. The Court denies counsel Ujvala Singh's request and adopts its tentative ruling.

Defendants' (HERBERT L. TERRERI, ESQ. and LAW OFFICES OF HERBERT L. TERRERI, APC) motion for leave to file a cross-complaint is granted.

The law disfavors forfeitures and there is a strong policy in favor of allowing a party to file a cross-complaint whether it is compulsory or permissive. Judicial economy is served by resolving a dispute in one action. The proposed cross-complaint against plaintiff (proposed cross-defendant) Optrics, Inc. is compulsory and Optrics, Inc. fails to make a strong showing of bad faith to warrant denial of this motion. (See CCP 426.50; Silver Organizations Ltd v. Frank (1990) 217 Cal.App.3d 94, 99 ["A motion to file a cross-complaint at any time during the course of the action must be granted unless bad faith of the moving party is demonstrated where forfeiture would otherwise result. Factors such as oversight, inadvertence, neglect, mistake or other cause, are insufficient grounds to deny the motion unless accompanied by bad faith."]; Foot's Transfer & Storage Co. v. Superior Court (1980) 114 Cal.App.3d 897, 902 ["We conclude that this principle of liberality requires that a strong showing of bad faith be made in order to support a denial of the right to file a cross-complaint under this section."] Optrics, Inc.'s arguments regarding the merits of the cross-complaint should be addressed by later motion or demurrer. (Silver Organizations Ltd., 217 Cal.App.3d at p. 102 ["[I]f there were merit to these contentions they might be appropriated raised by demurrer, summary judgment, or other proceedings after the compulsory cross-complaint has been filed"].)

Defendants re-served this motion on all other parties on September 20, 2024, so there is no longer an issue under CCP 1014.

Order signed.

Judge: Rochelle C. East; Clerk: V. Da Fonseca; Court reporter: Angela Pourtabib, CSR# 13714 (tel: 415-601-7054 / email: apourtabib@gmail.com). (302/RCE)
